gmail.description: Read inbound RFQ threads, draft outbound RFQ replies as Gmail drafts. Used by rfq-drafting and rfq-inbox-watcher.
gmail.fallback: Render output as plain text; operator copy-pastes.

google-drive.description: Save generated transport documents into the shipment folder structure. Used by transport-documents.
google-drive.fallback: Output document as .docx/.pdf attachment in chat; operator saves manually.

google-calendar.description: Block calendar slots for pickup windows, delivery appointments, charter ETD/ETA milestones.
google-calendar.fallback: Output the calendar entry as text; operator adds manually.

outlook.description: Same as gmail, for Microsoft 365 operators.
outlook.fallback: Render output as plain text; operator copy-pastes.

cargo-one.description: Live air-freight rate lookup. Used by rfq-drafting when an outbound rate is requested. Vendor-specific MCP server; see connectors/cargo-one/ for setup.
cargo-one.fallback: Skill skips rate suggestion; operator pulls rate manually.

cargowise.description: TMS read-only — pull shipment master data, check booking status, log new shipments after booking confirmation. See connectors/cargowise/ for setup.
cargowise.fallback: Skill outputs the booking confirmation as a draft; operator enters into TMS manually.

notes: All connectors are optional. The plugin works without any of them — output falls back to text the operator can paste. Recommended baseline for desk operators: gmail (or outlook) + google-drive. Add cargo-one or cargowise once the baseline is stable.
